name: Air Dash
description: A dash that can be activated while airborne.
design_notes: Air dash changes the shape of jumps, gaps, enemy attacks, and recovery. It should define reset rules carefully: landing, wall contact, pickups, and enemy bounces can all refill or withhold the action.
design_purpose: Support advanced traversal routes and combat repositioning.
Let designers build routes around committed aerial bursts.
player_fantasy: The player feels agile in midair, able to redirect momentum with confidence.

parameters.name: air_dash_distance
parameters.description: Aerial displacement target before collision truncation.

parameters.name: air_dash_vector_mode
parameters.description: Allowed direction model for the dash.

parameters.name: air_dash_reset_rule
parameters.description: Event that restores the air dash charge.

parameters.name: air_dash_hover_frames
parameters.description: Optional gravity pause before or during the burst.

edge_cases: Using air dash at the exact landing frame can spend ground dash or air dash depending on priority.
Collision during hover frames must not leave gravity disabled.
Air dash into one-way platforms should not snap the player upward unexpectedly.
common_bugs: Dash charge resets repeatedly on wall contact and enables unintended climbing.
Air dash uses ground friction and loses distance immediately.
Input direction is sampled after the character flips from target lock.
balancing_notes: Air dash plus double jump greatly expands reachable space and should be tested against existing levels.
Reset pickups can make air dash a route puzzle rather than a general escape option.
accessibility_notes: Use clear charge feedback so players know whether an air dash is available while airborne.

implementation_notes.unity: Use an explicit movement-state enum and feed the final desired velocity into CharacterController.Move or Rigidbody movement from FixedUpdate; keep dash and jump assists outside animation callbacks. For Air Dash, validate this path with parameters such as air_dash_distance and air_dash_vector_mode.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep the mechanic state on CharacterBody2D or CharacterBody3D, update velocity in _physics_process, and separate input buffering from collision response. For Air Dash, validate this path with parameters such as air_dash_distance and air_dash_vector_mode.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use CharacterMovement with a custom movement state or Gameplay Ability; gate activation through prediction-safe input and reconcile movement corrections cleanly in networked play. For Air Dash, validate this path with parameters such as air_dash_distance and air_dash_vector_mode.
implementation_notes.web: Represent the mechanic as deterministic state in the simulation loop, then apply resolved displacement before rendering through Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or a custom engine. For Air Dash, validate this path with parameters such as air_dash_distance and air_dash_vector_mode.
